title: History of winning remodels thalamo-PFC circuit to reinforce social dominance.

assets.abstract: Mental strength and history of winning play an important role in the determination of social dominance. However, the neural circuits mediating these intrinsic and extrinsic factors have remained unclear. Working in mice, we identified a dorsomedial prefrontal cortex (dmPFC) neural population showing "effort"-related firing during moment-to-moment competition in the dominance tube test. Activation or inhibition of the dmPFC induces instant winning or losing, respectively. In vivo optogenetic-base

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Bridge paper score=8.0: links Wind/Pride concept to mechanistic neuroscience
